baseline_nocontext_serial: Refactor the following serial {lang} code to optimize runtime performance by reducing execution time, while preserving all functionality. The refactored code must remain strictly serial. Do not introduce any parallelism.
refactorplan_serial:
Carefully follow all these instructions step-by-step: 
STEP 1. Read the background knowledge, your background knowledge summaries about programming and performance optimization strategies, and your description of the CPU system on which the code will be compiled and executed. 
STEP 2. Read your description and analysis of the source code's functions, data structures, and overall program flow.
STEP 3. Analyze inefficiencies in the CODE TO REFACTOR: identify redundant computations, inefficient memory usage, poor cache locality, suboptimal loop structures, inefficient algorithms or data structures, unnecessary function call overhead, and any other issues you find. 
STEP 4. Output a summary of any identified inefficiencies. 
STEP 5. Propose a code refactoring plan that applies your knowledge of the CPU system and optimizations and specific best practices from the background knowledge to reduce execution time during the code runtime. Avoid generating code in your refactoring plan.
refactor_serial:
Carefully follow these instructions step-by-step:
STEP 1: Select the most impactful of the improvements outlined in your refactoring plan that does not yet appear in the code below.
STEP 2: Rewrite the code to implement this one change with the intention to reduce runtime execution time.
 [REQUIREMENTS] Be sure to maintain all function names, parameters, and return types. Preserve all original outputs. Ensure the code compiles with {compiler} or standard {lang} compilers. The refactored code must remain strictly serial. Before generating the refactored code, briefly describe the changes you will make.
